mnemonics.title: Coffman Conditions Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER 'MHNC' - MUTUAL EXCLUSION, HOLD AND WAIT, NO PREEMPTION, CIRCULAR WAIT. OR USE 'COFFMAN' AS AN ACRONYM.

mnemonics.title: Prevent Deadlock Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: TO PREVENT DEADLOCK: DENY HOLD-WAIT (request all at once), ALLOW PREEMPTION, ENFORCE ORDERING (prevent circular wait). REMEMBER: H2O - HOld and wait, Preemption, circular wait REMOVAL.

acronyms.usage: Visual representation of system state showing processes and resource allocation/request relationshipsman Conditions

acronyms.expansion: Four necessary conditions for deadlock: Mutual exclusion, Hold and wait, No preemption, Circular wait
acronyms.usage: All four must hold simultaneously for deadlock to occur

visualTips.visualization: Draw circles for processes (P1, P2...) and squares for resource types (R1, R2...). Use arrows from process to resource for ALLOCATION, arrows from resource to process for REQUEST. A cycle in the graph with single-instance resources means deadlock.
Imagine a circle of people where each person holds something the next person needs, and each wants what the previous person has. No one can move - that's circular wait.

Picture a traffic intersection with 4 cars (processes) each blocking the others (mutual exclusion), each waiting for the car ahead to move (hold and wait), no tow truck coming (no preemption), and cars in a circle (circular wait).

analogies.analogy: Four cars arrive at a 4-way intersection simultaneously, each blocking the other. No car can move because each is waiting for another to move first - this is exactly how deadlock works in computer systems.
A person holding a library seat (holding) while looking for a better seat elsewhere (waiting) - blocking others from using the seat they hold.
Like trying to take away a loaded gun from someone - they won't give it up voluntarily, and you can't force them without potential problems.
Two people each holding one chopstick and waiting for the other chopstick held by the other person - classic deadlock in dining philosophers problem.
